steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The words before the blank complete an independent clause about what the Laffer curve can be used to determine. The words after the blank begin another independent clause: the 2017 data suggested a conclusion about South Korea.

steps.title: Reject fused-clause options
steps.content: A creates a comma splice, and B runs the two clauses together with no punctuation. C uses a semicolon before an as clause, so it does not join two independent clauses. A period cleanly separates the two complete thoughts. The answer is D.
